title: Dicraeopetalum mahafaliense
intro: Dicraeopetalum mahafaliense is a plant species native to Madagascar, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Dicraeopetalum mahafaliense include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as to make topical creams and ointments, allowing for its bioactive compounds to be absorbed and utilized for therapeutic purposes.
